notes.text: Reading Worksheet
Noun is a name of a person, place, animal or thing.
A noun is also called as naming word.
Proper nouns are name specific people, things, and places. They are always capitalized.
A common noun are names of a person, animal, place, or thing. A common noun starts with a lowercase letter, unless it is the first word in a sentence.
##### Example
a girl (Common Noun)
Riya (Proper Noun)
She is a girl. Her name is Riya.
a school (Common Noun)
Seth M.R. Jaipuria (Proper Noun)
Seth M.R. Jaipuria is a famous school.
food (Common Noun)
Maggi (Proper Noun)
My favourite food is Maggi.
a dog (Common Noun)
Bruno (Proper Noun)
Bruno is my pet dog.

notes.text: ##### Reading Worksheet
A singular noun is a noun that refers to only one person, place, animal or thing. Example-boy, school, dog, pen.
A plural noun is a noun that refers to more than one person, place, animal or thing. Example-boys, schools, dogs, pens.
Rules for changing singular noun to plural.
Rule 1- By adding 's'
Rule 2- By changing the word completely.
Rule 3- Nouns that remains the same in singular as well as in plural.

notes.text: Reading Worksheet
Gender: The gender of a noun tells whether the person or animal is male (masculine or female (feminine)).
Masculine: A noun that talks about a male is called the masculine gender.
Feminine: A noun that talks about a female is called the feminine gender.

notes.text: Reading Worksheet
A pronoun is a word which is used in place of a noun.
'This' is used for singular nouns which are close to us.
This is a book.
'That' is used for singular nouns which are far from us.
Example- That is a book.
'These' is used for plural nouns which are close to us.
These are books.
'Those' is used for plural nouns which are far from us.
Example- Those are books.

notes.text: ##### Reading Worksheet
A preposition is a word used before a noun or a pronoun to show the position of an object in relation to the other object.
Prepositions are of many kind.
For Example —
in/on/under/behind/infront of/between.
The boy is between the dogs.
The girl is under the table.
The cat is behind the box.
The apple is on the box.
The dog is in the box.
The ball is in front of the box.

5. The lamp is under/on the drawer.

notes.text: Describing words (Adjectives): An adjective is a word that is used to describe something about a noun. They are also called describing words.
Example: soft, warm, dry, big, tall, small, beautiful, funny, etc.
Rolo is a clown.
The dog jumped over the bar.

notes.text: Reading Worksheet
Verb A verb is a word that tells what someone or something is doing. A verb is also called a 'doing word' or an 'action word'.
Example: run, spin, sleep, fall, jump, climb, draw, watch, swim, etc.
##### Reading Worksheet
Verbs change their form according to the number of nouns they are used for.
Verbs used for yesterday are called the verbs in past tense.
Verbs used for today are called the verbs in the present tense.
Verbs which take '-ed' in the past form.
Verbs which change completely.
Verbs which do not change in their Past Tense

notes.text: Reading Worksheet
Conjunction: A conjunction is a word that joins two or more words, group of words
sentences together. 'and', 'but', 'because' are conjunctions.
We use 'and' to join two words or sentences.
Example- lock key
I have a pear. I have an orange.
I have a pear an orange.
We use 'because' to show reason.
Example - I am carrying an umbrella. It is raining.
I am carrying an umbrella because it is raining.

notes.text: ##### Facts and Opinions
A fact is a statement that is always true. It can be tested and proven.
Opinions are someone's feelings or beliefs about a particular topic. It varies from person to person.
Classify the following statements as fact or opinion
Write one fact and one opinion about your favourite animal.

questions.q: What did you learn in Facts And Opinions?
questions.hint: Read the notes first, then answer.

notes.text: All About Me Bag!
What do I put inside?
I need to decide.
Some things about me
For my friends to see!
What I collect, like or do
That makes me special, too!

notes.text: Quantifiers indicates the quantity of something.
much - There isn't rice left in the bowl.
many-Jack has friends in the society.
more - You like sugar in your milk than I do.
most-Most of the seats in the cinema hall were booked.
few-They went on the trip a days ago.
little - There is a milk in the refrigerator.
less-There is water in the glass.
some- children in the class went for a picnic.
a lot of- I have books in my bookshelf.
sequence the order of events.
To make a lemonade-
Firstly, you need to have some lemons, sugar and a pinch of salt with you.
Next, slice the lemons and squeeze them in a glass of water.
Then, add a table spoon of sugar to it.
After that, add a pinch of salt and stir it well.
Finally, garnish it with some mint leaves and your lemonade is ready.
Summer Holiday Homework

questions.q: There is ___ (article) pink Lotus in a ___ lake. (proper noun) There were two ___ (common noun) swimming in the ___ (common noun) (punctuation mark) A little ___ (common noun) comes to play near the lake. She falls into the lake. She cannot come out. She starts crying. ___ (article) kind woman hears her cry. She jumps into the lake and saves her. The happy girl says, 'Thank you, Aunty.'
